report.title: Nottingham Forest 0 - 0 Arsenal - Match Report
report.author: Josh James
report.description: We were held to a goalless draw away to Nottingham Forest for the second consecutive season, despite creating the better of the chances.
report.article_body:
We were held to a goalless draw away to Nottingham Forest for the second consecutive season, despite creating the better of the chances.

The first half didn’t produce a shot on goal, though both Gabriel Martinelli and Martin Zubimendi shot wide when well placed.

Matz Sels denied Bukayo Saka with a full-stretch save after the break, and we had a strong penalty appeal for handball turned down, but while we couldn’t find the breakthrough, the draw does extend our lead at the top of the table to seven points.

Our third in a run of four consecutive away games – in four different competitions – took us to the City Ground, with the chance to extend our lead at the top of the table up for grabs.

After an evenly contested opening quarter of an hour, we won a couple of corners in quick succession, one on either flank. The first fell to Martinelli at the far post after Sels got fingertips to the cross, but the Brazilian’s shot was charged down.

Ben White then shot over the bar from 20 yards after Madueke cut the ball back to him, while at the other end, David Raya raced out of his box to meet a throughball just ahead of Callum Hudson-Odoi.

The best chance of the opening half-hour fell to Martinelli. Just after White had forced a corner when his fizzing cross was turned behind by Ola Aina under pressure from Declan Rice, Martinelli found himself completely unmarked at the far post. Noni Madueke’s shot from the edge of the box somehow went through the crowd, where Martinelli was sliding in, but he stabbed it wide across the goal from close range.

Zubimendi was next to shoot wide after being set up by Madueke, as we looked to ramp up the pressure.

After the hosts had a positive spell of their own, a high, booming clearance from Martinelli bounced awkwardly for Murilo, allowing Viktor Gyokeres to run in on goal. The defender got back well though to deflect the Swede’s shot wide. 

It had been an engaging half, albeit one in which neither side managed a shot on target, and after the first 10 minutes of the second half passed without any further chances, Mikel Arteta turned to his bench and summoned Mikel Merino, Gabriel Jesus and Saka. 

The latter immediately fashioned a chance for Rice. Saka stole the ball off Neco Williams and swung in an early cross that Rice volleyed goalwards, but Sels was able to turn it behind. 

The reverse combination produced our next attempt on goal. Rice stood up a cross from the left that Saka met with a looping header, only for man of the match Sels to scramble across superbly and claw away.

Fellow sub Merino also made Sels work with a header aimed low down into the corner, then glanced another header wide as we entered the final 20 minutes.

Eberechi Eze was the final sub, and Jesus almost found him in the area following a long Raya clearance, but the ball wouldn’t come down for him in time. VAR checked a possible handball against Aina in the aftermath, but stuck with the on-field decision after a few replays, despite the defender seeming to move his arm towards the ball.

We remained frustrated after that, though, as we looked for the winner, recording back-to-back goalless draws in the league, but saw our lead at the top grow as Manchester City were defeated by the neighbours United. However, Aston Villa could cut our advantage to four points when they host Everton on Sunday.

We have had consecutive goalless draws in the Premier League for the first time since our opening two games of the 2012/13 season against Sunderland and Stoke City.

We didn’t face a single shot on target in a match for the fifth time in the Premier League this season, our most on record in a single campaign (since 2003/04) and the most by any side since Chelsea in 2020/21 (also 5).

Nottingham Forest have failed to score in 11 different Premier League games this season, more than any other side.

Forest failed to register a single shot on target in a Premier League match for the first time since November 2024 (v us at Emirates Stadium), and for the first time at home since April 2023 (v Manchester United).

Despite being substituted off at half-time, Gabriel Martinelli had the most shots (4) and second-most touches in the opposition box (6) of any player in this match.

Our fourth consecutive away game is this Tuesday evening, in Italy when we take on Inter Milan on matchday seven in the Champions League league phase looking to extend our 100% record in the competition. We return to Premier League action next weekend when we host Manchester United on Sunday.
